meta.title: C300 Petrol Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Mercedes C300 Petrol engines — M272, M276, M274, M264. Real UK costs, timing chain & piston insight, and honest reliability data. Get free quotes from 25+ vetted Mercedes specialists today.

@graph.name: Mercedes C300 Petrol Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a Mercedes C300 Petrol engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned C300 petrol engine costs £3,000–£6,000 supply only depending on which generation you have. Used engines are available from £1,200–£3,500. Mercedes C-Class engine replacements typically cost £3,500 or more. Fitting adds £1,000–£1,600 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000.

mainEntity.name: Is the Mercedes C300 Petrol engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on which generation you have. The M276 V6 (W204 facelift, 2011–2014) is widely considered the most reliable C300 petrol. The M272 V6 (W204 pre-facelift, 2008–2011) has intake manifold flap and balance shaft issues. The M274 turbo four-cylinder (W205, 2015–2021) has well-documented timing chain, cracked piston, and wrist pin issues. The M264 is improved but has camshaft adjuster and cooling system concerns.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Mercedes C300 Petrol?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues vary by engine. M272: Intake manifold flap failure and balance shaft wear. M276: Timing chain tensioner wear and oil leaks. M274: Timing chain problems, cracked pistons, wrist pin issues, and PCV system failures. M264: Camshaft adjuster issues and cooling system faults.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Mercedes C300 Petrol engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and which generation. Against a well-kept £18,000 W205 C300, a £4,000 recon is 22% — worth it. Against a tired £5,000 W204 example, the same recon approaches our 70% Mercedes threshold — get a scrap valuation first. The M276 W204 facelift is the most desirable for reliability.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Mercedes C300 Petrol engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. W204 pre-facelift (2008–2011): M272.947. W204 facelift (2011–2014): M276.957. W205 (2015–2021): M274 (early) or M264 (late). The chassis code and year are the easiest tells.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain on the C300, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative timing chain and tensioner replacement costs £800–£2,500. If ignored and the chain snaps, the engine is an interference design — pistons and valves collide, resulting in catastrophic engine damage. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil are the single most effective preventative measure. For the M274, also check for wrist pin/piston issues if you experience engine failure.

eraMap.title: The C300 PETROL THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original C300 — a naturally aspirated V6. Balance shaft wear and intake manifold flaps are the main concerns.
The improved V6 — more power, better economy. Timing chain tensioners and oil leaks are the known patterns.

rows.engineCode: M274 (2.0L Turbo I4) / M264 (2.0L Turbo I4)
rows.eraNote: The turbo four-cylinder era. M274 is prone to timing chain and piston issues; M264 is more reliable but has camshaft adjuster and cooling system concerns.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.h2: Mercedes C300 Petrol Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the C300 petrol engine family. Mercedes C-Class engine replacements typically cost £3,500 or more. Used M272 engines are available from approximately £1,200. Main dealer new engines can exceed £10,000.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a C300 petrol engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer). V6 engines (M272/M276) require more labour than four-cylinder engines (M274/M264).

problems.title: Timing Chain & Tensioner Wear (All Engines)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start (2–3 seconds), metallic ticking from the engine, engine warning light, grinding noise on startup, eventual catastrophic failure if ignored.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles; M274 chain lifespan approximately 80,000 miles (±20,000); M272 balance shaft issues can appear earlier.
problems.repairCost: £800–£2,500 (chain + tensioner replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.replacementCost: £3,000–£8,500 (if chain snaps and valves strike pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: Timing chain problems are the single most significant concern across the C300 engine family. The M272 has balance shaft wear issues on early models. The M276's timing chain tensioners are known weak points. The M274 is prone to timing chain problems. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain and tensioners inspected immediately. Regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: M274 — Cracked Pistons & Wrist Pin Failure
problems.symptoms: Engine failure, loss of power, misfire, engine warning light, metallic knocking from the engine, complete engine seizure.
problems.repairCost: £2,000–£4,000 (piston replacement / engine rebuild)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear knocking, stop driving immediately

problems.recommendation: The M274 engine has a well-documented issue with cracked pistons (particularly cylinder 1) and wrist pin failures. A US petition was filed attributing M274 engine failure to "wrong sized wrist-pins". Mercedes issued a service bulletin for wrist pin / piston problems on a specific engine number range. If you experience engine failure or knocking, do not drive the car — catastrophic failure is imminent.

failureLink.label: Read the full piston failure guide

problems.title: Intake Manifold Flap Failure — M272 V6 (W204 pre-facelift)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, reduced fuel economy, rattling noise from the intake manifold.
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,200 (intake manifold repair kit or replacement)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The M272 V6's intake manifold flaps are made of plastic and can break over time. When the plastic lever arm breaks, the internal flaps either get stuck open or stuck closed. This can cause performance issues and, in severe cases, debris can enter the engine. A repair kit is available. This issue affects the W204 pre-facelift C300 (2008–2011).

failureLink.label: Read the full intake manifold failure guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: C300 Petrol — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Repair kit available; cheaper than engine replacement
If caught early, pistons can be replaced; often requires full engine rebuild
Well-understood repair; prevent long-term engine damage
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This C300 Petrol? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-2011 M276 V6 (facelift W204) — the most reliable C300 petrol
* M274: Engine outside the affected wrist pin number range; documented timing chain service
* M264: Post-2018 build with cooling system service records
* Full Mercedes service history with evidence of regular oil changes using MB 229.5 spec oil
avoidIf: * M272: Cold-start rattle or intake manifold noise with no documented repair
* M274: Engine failure or knocking with no diagnosis; check engine number range for wrist pin issues
* M264: Cooling system faults or camshaft adjuster noise
* Any engine: Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The C300 Petrol badge spans three distinct eras of Mercedes engine philosophy — the smooth naturally aspirated V6 of the W204, the improved M276 V6 of the facelift, and the turbocharged four-cylinder era of the W205. The M276 is widely considered the most reliable of the three, while the M274 has well-documented timing chain, piston, and wrist pin issues. The M264 is improved but still has camshaft adjuster and cooling system concerns. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained C300 is a £15,000–£30,000 premium saloon; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare C300 petrol engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Mercedes C300 Petrol (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling, ticking, or grinding (timing chain or tensioner warning)
* Service History — including which engine era (M272/M276/M274/M264) and confirmed oil-change intervals using MB 229.5 spec oil
* Timing Chain Check — Any history of timing chain or tensioner replacement? (essential for all engines)
* Wrist Pin Check (M274) — Check engine number against the affected range for wrist pin/piston issues
* Intake Manifold Check (M272) — Any loss of power, rough idle, or rattling? (intake flap warning)
* Coolant Check (M264) — Any history of coolant loss or cooling system faults?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, piston, or cooling system issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
